Identity.History: Demetrius I Poliorcetes, “the Besieger,” was a Macedonian king and one of the most dramatic Successors of Alexander the Great. Son of Antigonus I Monophthalmus, he won fame through bold campaigns, naval victories, and enormous siege engines, especially during his failed siege of Rhodes in 305–304 BC. He later captured Athens and briefly ruled Macedon, but his ambition made him many enemies. Defeated by rival Successors, he surrendered to Seleucus I and died in captivity in 283 BC.
